findings.message: Agent body explicitly instructs use of run_shell_command to create the board message-bus directory, but run_shell_command is not declared in the frontmatter tools list; the mkdir call will fail at runtime
Board of Directors agent has no example invocations; users cannot see what a properly formatted board-meeting request looks like or what output to expect

model: inherit is not a valid model identifier; executor cannot determine appropriate model tier; likely intended to inherit from the calling agent but this convention is not supported in standard Claude Code frontmatter

escalateToBoard helper spawns 'claude --print --model opus /orchestrator-supaconductor:board-meeting {question}' and invoke_superpower() interpolates $superpower and $params from track metadata into a shell command without sanitization; a track name or goal containing shell metacharacters (backtick, semicolon, $()) could inject arbitrary commands

No example invocation blocks present; this is the most complex agent in the plugin (master orchestrator) yet users have no worked examples showing how to trigger it or what output to expect (applies to 11/15 agents — representative finding logged)
Two near-identical commands (brainstorm and brainstorming) targeting the same skill; creates discovery ambiguity and maintenance burden
No allowed-tools field in frontmatter; the harness cannot restrict tool access for this command (applies to 48/49 commands in this plugin — only representative finding logged)

Command body contains stale cross-reference to /loop-plan-evaluator; the canonical invocation for this command is /evaluate-plan (matching the file name); creates user confusion especially since both commands exist
Command body references legacy direct-file agent path format for loop-executor agent

Command body references the legacy .claude/agents/orchestrator-supaconductor:loop-planner.md path format; current Claude Code convention dispatches agents via the Task tool or Skill tool invocation, not by direct file path
Command body references legacy direct-file agent path format for task-worker agent
Report template placed inside commands/ directory with no YAML frontmatter; it cannot be invoked as a command and will confuse artifact discovery tools

Outbound curl call to GitHub API on every Claude Code session start to check for plugin updates; makes an unconditional network request to an external service with no caching, adding latency to each session start
Skill has no YAML frontmatter; both required name and description fields are absent; the skill is invisible to the Skill tool and cannot be discovered or invoked

Skill description summarizes the v3 feature set rather than describing triggering conditions; per writing-skills CSO guidance, descriptions that summarize workflow cause Claude to follow the description summary instead of loading and reading the full skill body

Agent body explicitly instructs use of run_shell_command to create the board message-bus directory, but run_shell_command is not declared in the frontmatter tools list; the mkdir call will fail at runtime — Phase 5 body: 'Use run_shell_command mkdir -p conductor/tracks/{trackId}/.message-bus/board/' — tool not in frontmatter tools list which only declares read_file and write_file

Skill has no YAML frontmatter; both required name and description fields are absent; the skill is invisible to the Skill tool and cannot be discovered or invoked — File opens with raw markdown heading '# Business Docs Sync' with no preceding YAML frontmatter block

Report template placed inside commands/ directory with no YAML frontmatter; it cannot be invoked as a command and will confuse artifact discovery tools — File opens with '# UI/UX Audit Report' heading with no YAML frontmatter; scanning commands/ yields no name or description field

Outbound curl call to GitHub API on every Claude Code session start to check for plugin updates; makes an unconditional network request to an external service with no caching, adding latency to each session start — Line 25: curl -s --max-time 3 "https://api.github.com/repos/${REPO}/releases/latest" — REPO is hardcoded to Ibrahim-3d/orchestrator-supaconductor; runs unconditionally on every SessionStart hook invocation

escalateToBoard helper spawns 'claude --print --model opus /orchestrator-supaconductor:board-meeting {question}' and invoke_superpower() interpolates $superpower and $params from track metadata into a shell command without sanitization; a track name or goal containing shell metacharacters (backtick, semicolon, $()) could inject arbitrary commands

Command body references the legacy .claude/agents/orchestrator-supaconductor:loop-planner.md path format; current Claude Code convention dispatches agents via the Task tool or Skill tool invocation, not by direct file path — Command body: 'Use agent at .claude/agents/orchestrator-supaconductor:loop-planner.md' — path uses legacy direct-file convention
Command body references legacy direct-file agent path format for task-worker agent — Command body references .claude/agents/orchestrator-supaconductor:task-worker.md — same stale-path pattern
Command body references legacy direct-file agent path format for loop-executor agent — Command body references .claude/agents/orchestrator-supaconductor:loop-executor.md — same stale-path pattern

Command body contains stale cross-reference to /loop-plan-evaluator; the canonical invocation for this command is /evaluate-plan (matching the file name); creates user confusion especially since both commands exist — Command file is named evaluate-plan.md but body instructs 'Use /loop-plan-evaluator' which is the old command name

model: inherit is not a valid model identifier; executor cannot determine appropriate model tier; likely intended to inherit from the calling agent but this convention is not supported in standard Claude Code frontmatter — Frontmatter field: model: inherit — not a recognized Claude model ID (expected sonnet, opus, haiku, or a concrete model string)

No allowed-tools field in frontmatter; the harness cannot restrict tool access for this command (applies to 48/49 commands in this plugin — only representative finding logged) — Frontmatter has no allowed-tools field; applies to all 48 commands with this same omission

No example invocation blocks present; this is the most complex agent in the plugin (master orchestrator) yet users have no worked examples showing how to trigger it or what output to expect (applies to 11/15 agents — representative finding logged) — Agent body contains extensive TypeScript pseudocode and orchestration logic but no ## Examples section or examples: frontmatter field with concrete invocation samples

Skill description summarizes the v3 feature set rather than describing triggering conditions; per writing-skills CSO guidance, descriptions that summarize workflow cause Claude to follow the description summary instead of loading and reading the full skill body — Description: 'Master coordinator for the Evaluate-Loop workflow v3. Supports GOAL-DRIVEN entry, PARALLEL execution via worker agents, BOARD OF DIRECTORS deliberation, and message bus coordination...' — summarizes capabilities instead of triggering conditions

Board of Directors agent has no example invocations; users cannot see what a properly formatted board-meeting request looks like or what output to expect — Agent body describes a multi-phase deliberation protocol but contains no examples: section in frontmatter and no ## Examples section in body

Two near-identical commands (brainstorm and brainstorming) targeting the same skill; creates discovery ambiguity and maintenance burden — commands/brainstorm.md and commands/brainstorming.md both invoke the brainstorming skill with identical behavior; no functional differentiation
